audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Saul persecution, Samaria mission, Simon Magus, Peter rebuke, angel/Spirit-guided Ethiopian eunuch encounter, Isaiah reading, baptism, Spirit snatching Philip.

verses.restored: Saul was approving of his killing. In that day great persecution happened against the assembly in Jerusalem, and all were scattered through the regions of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.
Devout men buried Stephen and made great lamentation over him.
But Saul was ravaging the assembly, entering house by house; dragging off both men and women, he was handing them over to prison.
Therefore those scattered went through announcing the good news of the word.
Philip, going down to the city of Samaria, was proclaiming the Messiah to them.
The crowds with one accord were paying attention to the things spoken by Philip, as they heard and saw the signs he was doing.
For many who had unclean spirits, crying with a great voice, were released; and many paralyzed and lame ones were healed.
There was much joy in that city.
A certain man named Simon had previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the nation of Samaria, saying himself to be someone great.
All were paying attention to him, from small to great, saying, This one is the power of the Divine called Great.
They paid attention to him because for a long time he had amazed them with magic arts.
But when they trusted Philip announcing good news concerning the reign of the Divine and the name of Jesus Messiah, they were baptized, both men and women.
Simon himself also trusted, and being baptized, he was continuing with Philip; beholding signs and great powers happening, he was amazed.
When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of the Divine, they sent Peter and John to them,
who, coming down, prayed concerning them that they might receive holy Spirit.
For he had not yet fallen upon any of them; they had only been baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus.
Then they laid hands upon them, and they received holy Spirit.
Simon, seeing that through the laying on of the apostles' hands the Spirit was given, brought them silver,
saying, Give me also this authority, so that on whomever I lay hands, that one may receive holy Spirit.
But Peter said to him, May your silver be with you into ruin, because you thought the gift of the Divine could be acquired through money.
There is not for you a part or share in this word, for your heart is not straight before the Divine.
Turn/realign therefore from this harmfulness of yours, and ask the Lord if perhaps the intention of your heart may be released to you.
For I see you are in bitter gall and bond of injustice.
Simon answered and said, You ask the Lord for me, so that nothing of what you have said may come upon me.
They therefore, having testified and spoken the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, announcing the good news to many villages of the Samaritans.
A messenger of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, Rise and go toward the south on the road going down from Jerusalem to Gaza; this is wilderness.
Rising, he went. Look, an Ethiopian man, a eunuch, a powerful official of Candace queen of Ethiopians, who was over all her treasury, had come to worship in Jerusalem.
He was returning and sitting in his chariot, and he was reading the prophet Isaiah.
The Spirit said to Philip, Go near and join yourself to this chariot.
Running up, Philip heard him reading Isaiah the prophet and said, Do you indeed know what you are reading?
He said, How could I, unless someone guides me? He invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
The passage of Scripture he was reading was this: As a sheep to slaughter he was led, and as a lamb before its shearer is silent, so he does not open his mouth.
In his humiliation, justice was taken away from him. Who will describe his generation? For his life is taken away from the earth.
The eunuch answered Philip and said, I ask you, concerning whom does the prophet say this, concerning himself or concerning another?
Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture, announced the good news of Jesus to him.
As they were going along the road, they came upon some water, and the eunuch says, Look, water. What prevents me from being baptized?
[Some later manuscripts add a confession of trust before baptism.]
He commanded the chariot to stand still, and they both went down into the water, Philip and the eunuch, and he baptized him.
When they came up out of the water, Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, for he went on his way rejoicing.
Philip was found at Azotus, and passing through, he announced the good news to all the cities until he came to Caesarea.
